properties.name: Community Information
properties.webBody:
This message is being issued for water quality at Merlynston Creek.

  • Water used to extinguish a factory fire on Nathan Drive, Campbellfield has entered Merlynston Creek. The water may contain chemicals and heavy metals that may pose risks to human and animal health. 
  • Do not enter or touch the water in Merlynston Creek between National Boulevard, Campbellfield and Jack Roper Reserve.
  • Keep pets away from the creek water and lake.  
  • Do not consume fish from Jack Roper Reserve. 

You should avoid contact with the water in Merlynston Creek, do not allow your pets to swim in or drink the water. 
This message was issued by Environment Protection Authority.

Unless the situation changes, this will be the last message issued for this incident.

Use multiple sources to stay informed:
  • For updates on water quality, visit: Check air and water quality
  • www.emergency.vic.gov.au.
  • VicEmergency Hotline - freecall 1800 226 226.
  • VicEmergency app.
  • Facebook or Twitter.
  • ABC Local Radio, commercial and designated community radio stations, or Sky News TV.

Accessibility:
The following services can help you, or someone you know, access information during an emergency.
  • For information in other languages call the Translating and Interpreting Service on 131 450 (freecall) and ask them to call the VicEmergency Hotline.
  • If you are deaf, hard of hearing, or have a speech/communication impairment contact the National Relay Service and ask them to call the VicEmergency Hotline.
properties.text:
COMMUNITY INFORMATION - WATER QUALITY - Stay Informed
Incident Location: Nathan Dr, Campbellfield
Issue Date: Thursday 11 June 2026, 04:14 pm
Next Update: Saturday 13 June 2026, 04:20 pm

More details at http://emergency.vic.gov.au/respond/#!/warning/41996/moreinfo

This message is being issued for water quality at Merlynston Creek. 

-   Water      used to extinguish a factory fire on Nathan Drive, Campbellfield has      entered Merlynston Creek. The water may contain chemicals and heavy metals      that may pose risks to human and animal health.   
-   Do not      enter or touch the water in Merlynston Creek between National Boulevard,      Campbellfield and Jack Roper Reserve.  
-   Keep      pets away from the creek water and lake.  
- Do not      consume fish from Jack Roper Reserve.   



You should avoid contact with the water in Merlynston Creek, do not allow your pets to swim in or drink the water.
